SDG 12

Responsible production and consumption practices are embedded in both campus operations and the academic curriculum. KHAS implements strict waste management protocols aligned with national regulations, separating paper, plastic, glass, metal, medical, and electronic waste. Recyclable materials are collected by licensed companies, and leftover food supports street animals. Courses and workshops on sustainability, circular economy, and social innovation raise awareness among students, while operational measures—such as optimizing catering services to prevent overproduction—reinforce the University’s commitment to responsible resource use.

Faculty of Art and Design | Unit/Center: Beykoz Kundura

Activity Type: Panel

Summary: Moderated and curated by Özlem Hemiş and Ayşe Draz, the panel explored Turkey’s theater landscape and the cultural policies shaping it. Prof. Dr. Dikmen Gürün presented perspectives on the formation of Turkey’s theater scene and the cultural policies influencing it, followed by discussions with artists whose works were supported by Beykoz Kundura’s educational and production programs.

Center for Liability Law Practice and Research.

Green Library Project: Implemented initiatives in waste management, water conservation, energy efficiency, and ensured free access to clean water through fountains and filtered dispensers.

Book Donation Campaigns (2023): 9,477 volumes donated to schools, penal institutions, universities, and municipalities; “gift-a-book” drives were organized with the participation of university members.

Book Donation Campaigns (2024): 8,609 volumes donated to ten beneficiary institutions, continuing the “gift-a-book” initiative.

Sustainability Practices in Education:

Educational materials were digitized, reducing the need for printing and encouraging sustainable resource use.

Paper consumption quotas were implemented for academic and administrative staff to prevent waste.

These practices contribute to energy and resource savings, supporting the university’s environmental sustainability goals. (School of Foreign Languages – YDYO)

OHS / Sustainability Projects (ISG Projects):

Kadir Has University has initiated a zero-waste management process by procuring the necessary equipment in line with the Zero Waste Regulation. Packaging waste is separated using triple-set bins. Paper-cardboard, plastic, metal, and glass waste are collected separately. Special waste, including used batteries, fluorescent lamps, and hazardous laboratory waste, is also collected and stored separately to ensure safe disposal.

Waste Management and Zero-Waste Certification

Kadir Has University has established a zero-waste system to reduce the amount of waste released into nature and to increase recycling rates. In 2024, basic-level Zero Waste Certificates were obtained for each campus building from the Provincial Directorate of Environment, Urbanization and Climate Change.

Recyclable Waste Management

The university systematically separates paper, glass, plastic, metal, and hazardous waste and monitors their recycling performance annually:

2023 Waste Recycling Distribution:

Paper waste recycling: 15%

Other recyclable waste (glass-plastic-metal-mixed): 66%

Recycled hazardous waste: 19%

2024 Waste Recycling Distribution:

Paper waste recycling: 64%

Other recyclable waste (glass-plastic-metal-mixed): 26%

Recycled hazardous waste: 10%

12.4. Courses

LAW 336 – Consumer Law (Elective)

Course Description: This course covers the fundamental principles and terminology of Consumer Protection Law, including defective goods and services, and the optional remedies available to consumers who acquire such goods and services. It also examines dispute resolution mechanisms in consumer law and the role of consumer organizations.

[1]

BUS437 – Sustainability and Ethics in Business

This course explores the principles of sustainability and ethical practices in business, focusing on corporate social responsibility (CSR), ethical decision-making, and the long-term environmental and social impacts of business operations.

BUS302 – Business Communication and Ethics

This course focuses on effective business communication strategies while integrating ethical considerations into professional interactions. Students develop skills in corporate correspondence, presentations, and ethical decision-making in workplace contexts.

Course: IAR306 – Interior Architecture Design Systems IV: Installation | Semester: Spring 2022–23 / 2023–24

Course Description: This course introduces environmentally conscious and sustainable construction approaches, covering environmental design, passive and active design strategies, mechanical and plumbing systems, technical infrastructure, and fire safety. It aims to equip students with a vision that incorporates environmental, economic, and social sustainability principles into professional practice.

Course: IAR356 – Sustainability and Built Environment | Semester: Fall 2023–24 / 2024–25

Course Description: This course introduces the fundamental concepts of sustainability in the design of the built environment, emphasizing an interdisciplinary approach that integrates environmental, economic, and social dimensions. Students analyze design decisions at the building, façade, space, and material scales, developing awareness of long-term impacts, resource optimization, and quality of life improvements.

Course: History of Industrial Design (Instructor: Ayşe Erek) | Semester: Spring 2023–24

Course Description: This course examines the history of industrial design from the 19th century to the present, focusing on the evolution of products, production methods, and design movements in a socio-cultural context.
